title: Hattie's Magical Rainbow Tape Quest
summary: Hattie the mouse leaves her workshop on a quest for magical rainbow tape to use in her hat-making. Her journey leads her to a shop on Poplar and Popcornioni Street, where Mischief Mouse directs her toward Pepperoni Street. Along the way, Hattie survives a squelchy encounter with a mud puddle, getting a much-needed cleaning from her neighbors Bert and Ernie. Upon reaching her destination, she discovers a wondrous store decorated with a red tape parrot. Guided by a clerk named Lily, Hattie finds the rainbow tape hidden in the vegetable aisle and returns home successful.
audioFilename: Stars and Stories - Hattie & the Magical Rainbow Tape.m4a

highlightQuote.text: Oh Bert and Ernie, Hattie just stepped in the mud puddle so you've got to sweep it up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you need to use the potty before stars and stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, can you put me—Dad, I want to sit in your lap and put the stars there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. There.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And put my knees...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Today story is always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. And can Jane be in today's story too?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What about Nana's bunny?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who else is in it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: How about Hattie and Izzy the kiddo are in the story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Just... just Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, just Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Um... once upon a time, Hattie the mouse set out from her workshop to go on an adventure to find something very special. It was—what?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A very special kind of tape that she could use to make hats. It was the magical rainbow tape. But she didn't know where to go to find it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, maybe the store that only has tape, and it's on Poplar and Popcornioni Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, yes. The store that only has tape on Poplar and Popcornioni Street is the first place that she went. She knocked on the door—knock knock knock—and the person that ran the store, the tape store... who runs the tape store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm. It's Mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse opened the door and said, 'Oh, hi Hattie, how can I help you today? Welcome to the tape store.' And Hattie the mouse said, 'I'm looking for the mysterious magical rainbow tape.' And Mischief Mouse said, 'Oh, well, I don't have the mysterious magical rainbow tape in my store. You're going to have to go on a long journey to find it. I wish I had some. If you find it and you bring some back, could I have some?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh no, there's one on a different street called Pepperoni Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pepperoni Street? said Mischief Mouse. Huh. Maybe that's where the magical rainbow tape is to be found.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep, it's there on that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, good luck. Now, Pepperoni Street was very far away. So Hattie the mouse had to decide—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's nearby your house, Hattie Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Is she going to—it's nearby her house, but it's still very hard to get to. Is she going to take an airplane or a hot air balloon?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, she's going to walk there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she's going to walk to Pepperoni Street. And so Hattie the mouse set out walking. Now where did she go first on the way to Pepperoni Street?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She just kept walking.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She walked down the street, walk walk walk. But suddenly something got in her way.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, nothing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, nothing got in her way and she kept going. And then she saw her friend. Who did she see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nobody. And so she kept walking and then... oh yuck, she stepped on something disgusting. What did she step on that went squish?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The mud! And then I called Bert and Ernie. Hattie just saw the mud.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who did you call?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I called Bert and Ernie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Bert and Ernie. And... well when Hattie stepped in the yucky mud puddle it made this sound—squish squish—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, Bert and Ernie!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Bert and Ernie, I stepped in some mud and it went squish.' And she happened to be standing in the mud puddle just beneath the window where Bert and Ernie lived in their house was just next to where she was standing in the mud puddle. And they opened up the window and poked their heads out, Bert and Ernie, and they said, 'Oh, good morning Hattie, what are you doing in that mud puddle?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh Bert and Ernie, Hattie just stepped in the mud puddle so you've got to sweep it up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh, sorry to hear that. Well, uh, yeah we can help you, we'll be right down in just a minute. I just need to check the time.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The star!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, I'll get the star. Let's see here. All right, just setting—I forgot to set the blue timer. Okay. And so Bert and Ernie closed their door and then thump thump thump thump thump they came down the stairs and they opened the front door—breeeeep—and they said, 'Oh look at that, you're all covered with mud Hattie. Should we hose you down or do you want us to wipe you up with some paper towels?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I can do it. I can wipe wipe wipe wipe wipe wipe.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, so Bert and Ernie both got some paper towels and some water and then they just... they wiped Hattie down so she was all clean and all better. And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I stepped in the mud puddle, so now you just sweep it up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we'll sweep it up. And Ernie said, 'I'll go get the broom' and he went and got the broom and Bert said, 'Hmm, I don't know about that Ernie, it's mud, I don't think you should sweep it.' And Ernie came down and tried to sweep the mud but it just squished and squashed all over the place and got the broom all muddy too. And Bert said, 'I think we need a different approach to clean up all this mud. I'm going to use the hose and spray everything down, including you Ernie, because you're all muddy.' And Ernie said, 'Oh all right' and he just sat right down in the mud puddle—splat—and Bert got the hose and sprayed everyone and everything until it was all clean and the water sprayed everything off.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Wow, that was amazing!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Bert and Ernie said, 'Thanks! Well, uh, nice to see you Hattie.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie, there's no more mud puddles, so you can keep walking now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Hattie kept walking because there was no more mud puddle. And then she walked down to the end of the street and she saw a street sign. What did the street sign say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They look both ways.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Look both ways before you cross. And did she cross the street?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She crossed the street and what did she find on the other side?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Pepperoni Street!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Pepperoni Street! And the first store that she saw in Pepperoni Street was not the special tape store, it was a different store. What was—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it was the tape store!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Actually it was the special tape store. And the special tape store was very special. The front of the store had a beautiful display of something amazing made out of tape. What was there in front of the store that was all made out of tape?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A decoration.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A decoration. It was a decoration of a big animal made of tape. What kind of animal was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a parrot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A parrot! Oh, and what colors was the parrot?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was red.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad Hank, while you tell story I'm going to take a little rest because my head hurts a little.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, your head hurts?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. So what I'm going to do is rest for a minute while you tell the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are you going to rest over there in the chair? Okay. And so Hattie admired the beautiful red parrot made out of tape and she opened the door to the special tape store and she stepped inside. And what was the first thing she saw?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some magical tape.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what color was the magical tape?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange, yellow, green, and blue, indigo, violet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was all those colors of tape. There were different rolls of different colors. There was orange magical tape and yellow and green magical tape and indigo and violet and blue and all the colors. But she didn't see the rainbow tape that was all the colors at once. And—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, it's on a different aisle. It's in the aisle of the lemon one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The lemon one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not the Lemon Street. The lemon... the rainbow tape is in the vegetable aisle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's in the vegetable aisle. Well, just then somebody who worked at the magical tape store came over to help Hattie. They said, 'Oh hi, welcome to the magical tape store, how can I help you?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Said... um, said it was Lily.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh it was Lily that worked at the magical tape store. That's right, Lily the mouse. And she said, 'Hi Hattie, how can I help you?' And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can't find rainbow tape.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said—oh, I know just where it is, let me help you. It's in this other aisle that also oddly happens to have vegetables in it, but it's right here. And she brought Hattie around the corner and there was a display covered with beautiful rainbow colored tape, every piece of tape had all the colors.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm done resting.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're done resting? Okay. Now you can finish telling the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. So then Hattie had found the magical rainbow tape and what did she do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She... she ran home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She brought it home. And that's the end of our story. Well, now it's time to turn off the stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wow.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Mischief
characters.description: Proprietor of the analog-scented tape store on the corner of Poplar and Popcornioni, this avian known as Velociraptorning is Hattie’s loyal companion and the devoted mother of Mistress Mouse. Whether aiding Oscar on his adventures from their shared burrow or tending her wall of magnetic spools, she remains a soul of simple pleasures—sketching in sun-drenched parks, sharing meals of corn, and admiring her daughter’s new hat with a prideful, artistic eye.

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Stepping from the quiet ink of a library book to join the trek into the deep dark, this classic figure from a shared basement apartment maintains the rhythmic precision and meditative focus of a sleep-story weaver. Formerly the host of grand pigeon galas who folded his roommate Ernie’s vibrant chaos into serene harmony, he now applies a caretaker’s grace to the encroaching shadows. Whether smoothing mud-stained hems into crisp geometries or sorting through the abyss’s chaotic clutter, he treats the exploration of the unknown as a soothing ritual—a singular, exacting discipline of folding the world’s wilder reaches back into a state of restorative order.

characters.name: Ernie
characters.description: Bert’s classic companion and roommate moves through their shared world with the jittery stealth of a mouse, their impatient haste betraying them with a trail of unintended squeaks as they assist in the search for the lost pearl. Often found in a futile pursuit of order—whether frantically sweeping thick mud or overwhelmed by a cascade of forgotten toys—this iconic figure remains a soul perpetually caught between domestic chaos and a sudden drenching from a garden hose. Yet, on the long journey for the treasure, their nervous energy settles into a rhythmic wonder; they trade the mess of the earth for the fixed, silent patterns of the cosmos, weaving evocative stories of the stars to guide their path.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

places.name: Hattie's workshop
places.description: The sunlit cradle of Hattie’s grand adventure, this workshop remains a sanctuary of craftsmanship and logic where she shapes her signature hats and untangles complex puzzles—now serving as a warm, vital refuge for the islanders after their home was lost to the frost.

places.name: Poplar
places.description: The location of Mischief's tape store.

places.name: Popcornioni Street
places.description: The location of Mischief's tape store.

places.name: Pepperoni Street
places.description: The street where the special magical rainbow tape can be found.

places.name: Special tape store
places.description: A store on Pepperoni Street featuring a tape parrot and a vegetable aisle.
